messages.content: Write me very long poem about Love!

The Architecture of the Infinite**

Before the dawn had learned to break,
Before the first vast morning woke,
There was a silence in the dark,
A solitary, unspoken spoke.
The universe was dust and drift,
A chaotic ocean, vast and black,
With no hand to hold, no eye to lift,
And nothing that could turn to look back.
But then a tremor in the deep,
A sudden shifting of the tides,
A promise that the stars would keep,
Before the ancient silence hides.
This is the origin of the flame,
Before the breath, before the name.

It starts not with a trumpet’s call,
But with a whisper in the blood,
A sudden crumbling of the wall,
Where once a fortress stoically stood.
It is the lifting of a veil,
The sudden clarity of sight,
Where once was shadow, now is pale,
And all the world is washed in light.
You look upon a face and find
A geography you somehow know,
A map etched deeply in the mind,
From lifetimes lost in long ago.
It is the shock of recognition,
The solved equation of the soul,
The beautiful collision
That makes the broken spirit whole.

It is the sudden falling still
Of all the noise within the head,
The quieting of the restless will,
The words that never need be said.
For in the presence of the beloved,
The chatter of the world recedes,
The anxious heart is soothed and moved,
And quiet love, like rain, proceeds.
It is the way the coffee cup
Is placed upon the table’s edge,
The way the morning light wakes up
To trace the curve of cheek and ledge.
It is the smallness of a gesture,
The thread that sews the hours through,
A silence that is far vaster
Than the grandest word could ever do.

Then comes the season of the bloom,
The wild, erratic, beating drum,
The rose that bursts within the room,
The golden age that has come.
This is the love that poets praise,
The fire that consumes the air,
The labyrinth of endless days
Where two are caught within the snare.
It is the hunger and the feast,
The agony and the delight,
The rising from the east,
The burning of the midnight light.
To touch is to transcend the skin,
To kiss is to inhale the soul,
To lose the self you labored in,
And finally, completely, become whole.
Every street becomes a memory,
Every song a sacred hymn,
A private, perfect history
That grows and grows and does not dim.

But love is not a static thing,
A statue carved in marble stone,
It has a wild and beating wing,
A seed that must be sown and grown.
And so the seasons start to turn,
The initial fire settles down,
The passionate and fevered burn
Becomes a warmth within the town.
This is the test of time and tide,
When youth has fled and beauty paled,
When side by side you both abide,
And see the map of time is trailed.
It is the patience of the root,
The holding on when storms arise,
The refusal to refute
The light that lives within the eyes.
It is the laundry and the bills,
The traffic and the mundane day,
The quiet conquering of hills
In a less dramatic, deeper way.

There is a danger in the deep,
A risk in giving all you are,
For love can lull the heart to sleep
Or leave a jagged, lasting scar.
To love is to be vulnerable,
To walk without a shield or vest,
To be completely capable
Of being broken, being blest.
It is the trust you place in hands
That have the power to crush and break,
The anchor in the shifting sands,
The risk you willingly partake.
For in the opening of the chest,
Exposing every nerve and vein,
You put the spirit to the test,
And walk through joy and walk through pain.
But oh, the safety that is found
When safety is the gift you give,
A circle of protected ground
Where two have learned to truly live.

And what of when the shadows lengthen?
When hair turns silver, snow, and gray?
When the trembling hands can scarcely strengthen
The hold upon the fleeting day?
Then love becomes a steady lamp,
A guiding light against the dark,
A walk beside the marsh and damp,
A ember glowing in the park.
It is the comfort of the known,
The history written in a touch,
A kindness that the years have loaned,
A grace that does not ask for much.
It is the "I remember when,"
The shared joke that needs no voice,
The peace that settles over men,
The final, resolute choice.
You see the youth within the age,
The spark behind the fading eye,
The turning of the final page,
Without a fear, without a sigh.

And should the time come to depart,
When one must cross the dark alone,
Love creates a bridge for the heart,
A path distinct from dust and bone.
For love does not submit to death,
It is not bound by breath or beat,
It outlives the body's breath,
It is the form, the force, the heat.
The echo in the quiet room,
The impression on the pillow’s side,
The flower that continues to bloom,
The tide that moves the ocean wide.
It lives in those who stay behind,
In the lineage and the bloodline,
In the peace that they will find,
In the spark that makes the stars shine.
It is the thread that does not break,
The story told and told again,
The giving for another's sake,
The beginning that has no end.

So let this be a testament,
To the force that moves the sun and moon,
The hours in the heavens spent,
The silent, steady, cosmic tune.
It is the reason and the rhyme,
The architecture of the soul,
The master of the space and time,
The only thing that makes us whole.
From the first glance across the room,
To the final breath on the pillow’s edge,
From the first burst of the bloom,
To the commitment of the pledge.
It is the journey and the start,
The destination and the way,
The beating of the human heart,
The light that never fades to gray.
A vast and infinite expanse,
A
